steps.title: Pick the right model family from the spread of points
steps.content: The data climbs in roughly even steps — from near $(1200, 800)$ up through $(3200, 3100)$ and on to $(4100, 4100)$. The increase looks linear (no upward curl), so the right model is one of the linear choices, A or B. Choices C and D are exponential and don't match.

steps.title: Pick the right sign for the slope
steps.content: $y$ clearly increases with $x$, so the slope must be positive. Choice B uses $-1.2x$ (negative slope) and is eliminated. That leaves choice A: $y = -694 + 1.2x$.

steps.title: Verify choice A against a data point
steps.content: Use the point near $(3200, 3100)$. Plugging into $y = -694 + 1.2x$: $$y = -694 + 1.2(3200) = -694 + 3840 = 3146,$$ very close to the observed $\approx 3100$. The fit is good. That matches choice A — A is correct.
